description_long: This guard stone is a vertically oriented, rectangular stone slab with intricate carvings on its front face, depicting a stylized humanoid figure with a prominent headdress and detailed body features. The front surface is highly textured, with relief elements that suggest cultural or historical significance, possibly from an ancient civilization. The sides are relatively thin and show the depth of the relief, while the back is flat and unadorned, indicating it was likely installed against a wall or embedded in the ground. The stone appears weathered, with some surface roughness and minor chipping, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. The coloration is predominantly a light beige or tan, with some reddish-brown hues visible, possibly from mineral deposits or staining. This object would serve as a protective or decorative barrier in an urban or historical setting, marking boundaries or entrances.
description: A carved rectangular stone slab with a humanoid figure in relief, featuring a textured front and flat, unadorned back, showing signs of weathering and historical use.
description_view_0: The front view displays the detailed carved humanoid figure and ornate headdress, with the most intricate relief work visible.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the thin profile of the stone and the depth of the relief, with some protruding carved elements.
description_view_2: The back view is flat, featureless, and smooth, showing no carvings or decorations.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the slab's thinness and the side profile of the relief carvings.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to historical sites, along urban sidewalks, or at the corners of streets to protect buildings and mark boundaries.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed fully above ground, standing upright, often partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface chipping and erosion, especially on protruding carved areas, likely caused by weathering, occasional vehicle contact, and foot traffic. Discoloration and smoothing are present on the upper and front surfaces due to prolonged exposure.
attribute_historical_markings: The front face features intricate relief carvings of a humanoid figure and headdress, but no visible inscriptions or written symbols.
